scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from date fixed for performance, or if no date fixed, when plaintiff has notice that performance is refused. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into a written Agreement to Sell regarding the residential property bearing Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), as per the terms recorded in the agreement marked as Annexure A.
5. Pursuant to the said agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakhs Only) to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654, the receipt of which is acknowledged in Annexure B.
6. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the final Sale Deed.

BREACH PARTICULARS

7. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the Sale Deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the suit property, as evidenced by Annexure C.
8. To compel performance, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed in terms of the agreement, copy of which with postal receipt is filed as Annexure D.
9. Despite the said notice, the Defendant failed to perform his obligation under the agreement and did not comply with the notice to execute the Sale Deed.

DAMAGES

10. The Defendant's breach has caused direct financial loss to the Plaintiff by blocking the invested advance and preventing the acquisition of the property; the Plaintiff quantifies the value of the specific performance sought at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), being the total consideration under the agreement, and claims costs of the suit as the direct and natural consequence of the breach.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

12. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

13. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for specific performance to accrue.

14. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

15. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

16. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree of specific performance directing the Defendant to perform and complete the agreement dated 10.01.2024 in favour of the Plaintiff;
(b) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the necessary conveyance / sale deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration;
(c) In the alternative, award compensation / damages in lieu of specific performance;
(d) Award costs of the suit;
(e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 16 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Anita Sharma

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director: Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its registered office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner: Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value of {{AMOUNT}} as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. {{MEDIATION_STATUS -- CONFIRM: (a) Was pre-institution mediation conducted? If yes, provide mediation authority, date, and outcome certificate. (b) If not, is urgent interim relief being sought? Section 12A compliance is mandatory for commercial suits unless urgent interim relief is sought.}}

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system, with a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. The said Agreement is annexed hereto as Annexure P-1.

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff duly performed its obligation by paying the first milestone amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant via NEFT on 01.02.2024 (UTR: ICIC20240201456789). The NEFT receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure P-2.

8. Under the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system to the Plaintiff on or before 01.05.2024.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024 and remained in default regarding the delivery schedule.

10. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the development work, as evidenced by the email correspondence between the parties. The email correspondence is annexed hereto as Annexure P-3.

11. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant calling upon them to perform their obligations and return the advance payment. The legal notice is annexed hereto as Annexure P-4.

12. The Defendant failed to comply with the contents of the legal notice and did not return the advance payment or resume the development work.

DAMAGES

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to a refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- made to the Defendant, as the Defendant failed to provide any consideration against the said payment.

14. Due to the non-delivery of the ERP module and the subsequent abandonment of the project, the Plaintiff suffered consequential losses amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- on account of delay in operations, as assessed by the auditor. The loss assessment report is annexed hereto as Annexure P-5.

15. The total loss suffered by the Plaintiff is Rs. 32,00,000/-, which is the direct and natural consequence of the Defendant's breach of contract, and the Plaintiff claims the entire sum as damages.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline, thereby breaching the contract and causing the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

19. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

21. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
    (b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for a sum of Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) towards damages for breach of contract as particularised in the Schedule of Damages annexed hereto;
    (b) Award pendente lite and future interest at such rate as the Court deems just under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (c) Award costs of the suit including costs of the legal notice;
    (d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Refund of advance payment: Rs. 20,00,000/-
    Basis: Non-delivery of contracted services
    Documentary proof: Software Development Agreement, NEFT receipt

(ii) Consequential damages: Rs. 12,00,000/-
    Basis: Delay in operations due to non-delivery
    Documentary proof: Loss assessment report by auditor

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure P-1 — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure P-2 — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure P-3 — Email correspondence showing project abandonment

Annexure P-4 — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure P-5 — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Priya Menon, the Director and aut
